item.title: Re: Asking
item.content:encoded:
Timewatcher, it's generally a good idea to send a wikidot PM to the author of a work if you want to reference their writing in your own material. There's no guarantee they watch the discussion threads of their pages closely.

May I use scp-2802 for a scp I'm working on, because its a predator humaniod and i wanted to ask

Why 2802 in particular? Again, it's ideal to take the time to give some reasoning for the request beyond "so we don't fight over it".

item.title: Asking
item.content:encoded: May I use scp-2802 for a scp I'm working on, because its a predator humaniod and i wanted to ask just so there are no fighting you can see the first draft if you want

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've made some changes based on feedback; namely, I cut the administrative memos, added a different sort of addendum, and updated the containment procedures slightly. Hopefully these are improvements.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
If the administrator gave an actual reason why the birds were allowed to remain uncontained then this would be an easy upvote. The problem is that he doesn't. The Foundation doesn't let Safe skips roam around if they pose no threat. If it's anomalous and it can be contained, it will be contained.

-1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I agree with you on this, but the anatomic description felt detailed and well-tought enough for me to upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's the final note that killed it for me. The Foundation has much safer things underneath much more stringent security. The only things allowed to exist in the wild are those we cannot catch or move.

The main predators of the woodpecker include wild cats, foxes, rats. snakes and large birds although many other species of animal will prey on the woodpecker too.

You're ruling out that groups of predators dying from facial and digestive acid burns might cause notice from external groups. Due to both concentration and breeding rates, their displacement of actual prey birds in the local ecosystem would have drastic ramifications. Sudden introduction of lethal prey would easily wipe out predator populations. This would lead to explosion in other prey animals, or an explosion of woodpeckers (once they are no longer preyed upon, if biting one isn't lethal).

At what point do they develop their acidic cocktail? Many predators specialize on eggs and chicks. If they're poisonous as eggs, the impact is going to be more severe.

But a Project Administrator who blew off a founded, data-backed researcher's concerns would not be an administrator for long. If the replacement rate of the imitated species is as fast as you imply, this could cause a major ecosystem disaster, leading to a massive violation of the Status Quo.

-1. The horror is there. The Foundation isn't.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Kept waiting in excitement for a super subtle punchline about it having impersonated another species before, and possibly a third (HUMANS 3 SPOOKY 5 ME) in the future, but it didn't come. Upvote anyway.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This makes me sad. All of the birds dying out is an upsetting prospect.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What are birds? We just don't know.

Other than giving me reason to quote Look Around You, I kind of wonder what the point of this is. It seems to be pushing beyond "the Foundation extinctified the Ivory-Billed", but to what end, I'm not sure.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
o… kay.
um, upvote I guess
Not really sure what's going on here, but it's weird.
